name: Acrylic Painting (Adults) with Amy Kinzett Wednesday Nights
description: An 8-week adult acrylic painting course led by artist and art teacher Amy Kinzett. Participants learn and experiment with a range of painting styles, including abstracts. Classes are described as welcoming and relaxed, aimed at likeminded creative people.

name: Kick Back Fridays at Crooked River Estate
description: A weekly Friday evening event at the Crooked River Estate Cellar Door featuring live music and a casual food and beverage menu with estate-grown wines, craft beers, and cocktails. Seating at French and American oak barrels or on the hillside overlooking the vines (weather permitting). Pet friendly with EV charging available.

name: Smart Expressions 2026
description: Willoughby City Council presents an exhibition of student artworks selected from the 2025 NSW HSC practical examination in Visual Arts. Featuring works from six local high schools, the exhibition explores themes including immigration, poverty, mental health, identity, patriarchal values, and technological advancement.

name: Afterglow
description: S. Asher Gelman's internationally acclaimed play makes its Sydney debut. When married couple Josh and Alex invite Darius into their open relationship, an unexpected connection forms. A raw, funny, and sensual exploration of polyamory, commitment, and modern love. Contains strong language and nudity; recommended for ages 16+.

description: A live performance by Nine Year Sister, a multi-award-winning independent pop/folk duo from Queensland comprising singer-songwriters Jennie Wardle and Emma Wardle. Known for heartfelt lyrics, beautifully constructed melodies, and angelic harmonies. Supported by Arts North West.

name: Married for Six Weeks at the Newcastle Fringe
description: A solo comedy show by Sydney comedian Mandy Scotney about her tumultuous six-week marriage. Honest and hilarious, the show explores whirlwind romance, ignoring red flags, and navigating the aftermath. A RAW State finalist and shortlisted for Best in Comedy at the Sydney Comedy Fringe.

name: The Routine at Newcastle Fringe
description: Described as 'Death of a Salesman meets Mr. Bean', this surreal physical comedy follows a lonely office worker who squeezes through a portal in their bathroom mirror and goes on a quest for the meaning of life. name: Bach Akademie Australia – Bach: A Life in Music
description: Music in the Regions presents Bach Akademie Australia on their regional NSW tour. Founded by Australian violinist Madeleine Easton in 2016, the ensemble of internationally recognised instrumentalists and singers brings the musical story of J.S. Bach to life in an accessible and joyful way.

name: Handa Opera on Sydney Harbour – The Phantom of the Opera
description: Andrew Lloyd Webber's globally iconic Phantom of the Opera returns to Opera Australia's spectacular floating stage on Sydney Harbour to kick off the musical's 40th anniversary global celebrations. Directed by Simon Phillips with sets and costumes by Gabriela Tylesova, the production features a live orchestra hidden beneath the stage, nightly fireworks at approx. 8:45pm, and themed pop-up bars and dining against a backdrop of the Harbour Bridge and Opera House. Led by Jake Lyle (The Phantom), Amy Manford (Christine Daaé), and Jarrod Draper (Raoul). Audio described performance: 8 April; captioned performance: 14 April.

name: Sydney Royal Easter Show
description: One of Australia's most iconic annual events, running since 1823. Across 12 days, the Easter Show celebrates Australian agriculture, food, creativity and entertainment. Highlights include livestock competitions, woodchopping championships, the Imagine a Horse show, K9 Superwall Challenge, freestyle motocross, and nightly fireworks at 8:50pm. New for 2026 is the Yum Yum Noodle Market (Asian street food), the Ignite the Magic illuminated animal lantern parade, and David 'The Bullet' Smith's human cannonball act. Over 850,000 attendees expected.

name: Erykah Badu – Live in Sydney
description: Grammy-winning neo-soul icon Erykah Badu performs live at TikTok Entertainment Centre (formerly Qudos Bank Arena) as part of her Australian tour.

name: Wine & Cheese Fest Sydney
description: Making its Sydney debut having launched in Melbourne in 2014, the Wine & Cheese Fest is an all-day tasting event bringing together boutique wineries, artisan cheesemakers, and gourmet producers. Attendees enjoy unlimited tastings throughout the day, free masterclasses, live DJ entertainment, and receive a take-home wine glass and tote bag.

name: Sydney Comedy Festival 2026
description: Celebrating its 21st anniversary, the Sydney Comedy Festival presents its biggest program yet with over 350 artists from Australia and around the globe. New for 2026 is a 'One Night Stand' series at the Sydney Opera House — launching with Daniel Sloss on 24 April — plus the Comedy Crawl through the YCK precinct in the CBD. The lineup features international acts like Guy Montgomery, Jimeoin, and Arj Barker alongside Australian favourites including Rhys Nicholson, Melanie Bracewell, Nazeem Hussain, and Becky Lucas.

New for 2026 is a 'One Night Stand' series at the Sydney Opera House — launching with Daniel Sloss on 24 April — plus the Comedy Crawl through the YCK precinct in the CBD. The lineup features international acts like Guy Montgomery, Jimeoin, and Arj Barker alongside Australian favourites including Rhys Nicholson, Melanie Bracewell, Nazeem Hussain, and Becky Lucas.

name: Disney's The Lion King
description: Disney's The Lion King makes its triumphant return to Sydney for a third Australian production — the first since 2013. Directed by Julie Taymor with choreography by Garth Fagan and music by Elton John and Tim Rice, the cast features Aphiwe Nyezi as Simba, Nick Afoa as Mufasa, Emily Nkomo as Nala, and Buyi Zama reprising Rafiki for her third Australian tour. Over 120 million people worldwide have seen this six-time Tony Award-winning show.

name: Sydney Autumn Racing Carnival
description: One of the southern hemisphere's most prestigious racing series, the Sydney Autumn Racing Carnival features six spectacular racedays with over $46 million in prize money across six weeks. The carnival alternates between Royal Randwick and Rosehill Gardens, attracting world-class horses, trainers, and fashionable crowds.

name: Anzac Day Dawn Service & March
description: Australia's national day of remembrance honours those who served in the armed forces. The flagship Sydney ceremony is held at Martin Place with a Dawn Service attended by thousands, followed by a march through the city's streets along George Street. A public holiday — two-up is traditionally played at pubs across the city after the formal proceedings.
